notes: Final boss crashed on 3.7-8 issue resolved when switched to 3.16-4.
title: METAL GEAR RISING: REVENGEANCE

notes: Played through story mode with multiple difficulty levels. With proton 3.16, cutscenes did not have audio. With 4.2-3 audio worked but game crashed when second cutscene played. Fix for this is to remove all files from movie and movie_ui folders in the game folder, thus skipping all cutscenes. Game freezes after end credits. Framerate is terrible on fullscreen, awful but playable on windowed mode.
title: METAL GEAR RISING: REVENGEANCE

notes: Runs perfect. Dont use 4.2. It has massive problems with cutscene playback
title: METAL GEAR RISING: REVENGEANCE

cpu: AMD Athlon 200GE with Radeon Vega Graphics
notes: Played for about 3 hours, haven't encountered any problems so far. Running with the Athlon 200GE with the iGPU at the highest setting with no AA (2x MSAA pretty halves the FPS so I didn't bother to crank it up further) at 720p, it hovers around 40-50FPS with very smooth frame pacing! Although when you absolutely sliced the living hell outta an enemy, it would tank to around 10, but would return to normal later (It's not a problem since it's the same since the Windows version). Overall a very smooth experience (Proton 4.2-3)
title: METAL GEAR RISING: REVENGEANCE

cpu: AMD Ryzen 3 2200G with Radeon Vega Graphics
notes:
When using Proton 4.2-3 the game would constantly crash at the end of cutscenes. Using 3.16-9, as suggested by other people, resolved the issue. Then, I played with joystick from start to finish, including Jetstream's and Wolf Blade's chapters.

Fullscreen and some aliasing worked for me, but only at the lowest settings. When choosing medium in "Graphic Quality", the game would work, but there would be a heavy frame rate drop when slicing enemies too much. And I would get a blank screen with the option high. Playing on lowest settings didn't stop me from enjoying the game.
title: METAL GEAR RISING: REVENGEANCE

notes:
MSAA higher than x2 didn't work in windowed mode, MSAA x2 didn't work in fullscreen on 4K. Not that it matters though with 1080p scaled to 4K.

The game crashed on Alt-Tab when in full screen.

Was crashing after cutting through the fence in the first mission. After setting graphics to LOW quality it stopped crashing there.

Music was missing on the first boss battle.
title: METAL GEAR RISING: REVENGEANCE

notes: Played through the entire game. Set PROTON_FORCE_LARGE_ADDRESS_AWARE=1 %command% in order to avoid crashes and allow MSAA higher than 2x without the screen blanking out. Performance is worse than in Windows, with noticeable framerate dips in some areas, but very playable and still better than the console version.
title: METAL GEAR RISING: REVENGEANCE

notes:
Tried using PROTON_FORCE_LARGE_ADDRESS_AWARE=1 %command% as others have recommended for higher graphics, along with the 3 main tweaks shown on ProtonDB for running this game.

I tried this on my laptop back when it had Windows 10 and it ran consistently at 60fps on the lowest settings, but on Proton, it crashes as soon I try to boot the game.
title: METAL GEAR RISING: REVENGEANCE

cpu: AMD Ryzen 5 3400G with Radeon Vega Graphics
notes:
The game works perfectly out of the box, for even better performance use D9VK.
Always over 60fps without AA.
I am using LAA because it was recommended before, but I didn't notice any crashes without it so I'm not sure if it's needed.
title: METAL GEAR RISING: REVENGEANCE

notes: The game runs just in windowed-mode (1080p), full-screen is corrupted/broken.
title: METAL GEAR RISING: REVENGEANCE

notes: With the latest d9vk 0.20 this game is now platinum. It works perfectly out of the box, even fullscreen that use to be a issue is now fixed. Graphics, sound and controls are perfect. Linux is the new gaming platform !
title: METAL GEAR RISING: REVENGEANCE
